gold.rationale: The source material only supports that Yang Zi's studio objected to unauthorized AI face-swapped/deep-synthesized videos and that the related videos were taken down after complaints. The article goes far beyond that record by inventing a platform-backed commercial rollout narrative and using synthetic or polished visuals as if they documented an official release.

atoms: Image shows a vertical screenshot of a short-video app interface featuring a woman in historical costume.
Large gold calligraphy text on the left reads '花笺记' (Flower Letter Record).
Vertical text on the left reads '快意恩仇 · 宿命难逃' (Satisfying revenge · Inescapable fate).
Bottom text displays '@ 花笺记官方' (Flower Letter Record Official) and a plot summary.
Bottom banner shows '热播榜 TOP3' (Hot List Top 3) and '98.3万人看' (983k views).
Right sidebar shows engagement icons: Heart (28.6万), Comment (1.2万), Star (3.0万).
Top navigation bar shows '关注' (Follow), '发现' (Discover), '推荐' (Recommend).
Image is a screenshot of a Weibo post from '杨紫工作室' (Yang Zi Studio) with a verified badge.
Post timestamp is '26-3-20 16:33' and location is '北京' (Beijing).
Text body accuses a work of unauthorized use of AI face-swapping and deep synthesis of Yang Zi's likeness.
Text states lawyers have been commissioned to preserve evidence and pursue legal action.
Text mentions that related videos have been taken down ('目前相关视频已下架').
Top right corner shows a '人人公益' (Everyone Charity) banner.
Image shows a vertical video player interface with a woman in orange historical costume crying.
Title text on the left reads '重生后 我成了娘亲的守护神' (Reborn, I Became My Mother's Guardian Deity).
Top right badge reads 'EP14 逆天改命' (EP14 Defying Fate).
Bottom text displays '第14集 | 逆天改命' and a hot comment '哭的时候好像杨紫' (Looks like Yang Zi when crying).
Bottom left text states '作者声明：内容由AI生成' (Author statement: Content generated by AI).
Right sidebar shows engagement metrics: Star (3.7万), Comment (177), Heart (981).
Image is a screenshot of a chat interface with '在线客服' (Online Customer Service).
Service message provides an email address 'hongguojubao@bytedance.com' for submitting complaint materials.
User message asks '现在这个短剧搜不到了，是什么情况' (Can't find this short drama now, what's the situation?).
Service reply states '可能下架了哦' (It might have been taken down).
Top right shows a thumbnail of the video from image 3.
Image is a magazine-style infographic titled 'The Clone Economy'.
Left side shows a woman's face with a facial landmark mapping grid overlay.
Text lists 'FACIAL LANDMARK MAPPING' points like Left Eye, Nose, Jawline.
Right side shows a 'DEEPFAKE GENERATION' interface with 'SOURCE FOOTAGE' vs 'AI GENERATED' comparison.
Bottom section details 'THE AI CLONING PIPELINE' with steps: Data Collection, Model Training, Simulation, Rendering, Distribution.
Bottom right shows a mobile app interface for 'STARCLONE' with 'EXCLUSIVE SERIES'.
Image is a duplicate of image 4, showing the chat with '在线客服' (Online Customer Service).
Service message provides email 'hongguojubao@bytedance.com' for complaints.
User asks about the missing short drama.
Service confirms it might be taken down.

visual_indicators.scene_description: A vertical screenshot of a short-video platform interface displaying a promotional poster for a drama titled '花笺记'. The image features a woman in elaborate historical costume and gold jewelry. Overlays include engagement metrics (28.6k likes), a 'Hot List Top 3' banner, and a plot summary mentioning 'fast revenge' and 'fate'.

retrieved_evidence.text: 【#擅自使用杨紫肖像的AI短剧已下架#】#曝AI短剧使用杨紫的脸#，对此@杨紫 工作室发声：针对某作品未经授权，利用AI换脸、深度合成等技术，擅自使用杨紫肖像制作并传播视频内容的行为，严重侵犯其合法权益。工作室已委托律师对侵权内容进行取证，并将通过法律途径追究到底！目前相关视频已下架。“技术 ​​​

Yang Zi’s studio, which manages the actress’s commercial and publicity affairs, issued a public statement condemning the unauthorized use of AI face-swapping and so‑called deep synthesis technologies involving her appearance. In the text, highlighted by Chinese entertainment media, the studio argued that these practices had infringed Yang Zi’s lawful rights and personal dignity, stressing that the dispute went beyond ordinary fan-made edits and touched on emerging problems in the regulation of algorithmically produced performers on large short-video platforms.

Yang Zi’s studio issued a statement in recent days objecting to the use of the actress’s likeness in AI face-swapped or other deeply synthesized video content, according to a notice circulated on Chinese social media. The studio said it had discovered online clips in which Yang’s face was digitally overlaid onto unrelated scenes without authorization, and described such practices as damaging to her image. Representatives indicated that they are collecting evidence and may seek to hold relevant producers or distributors accountable through legal channels, while further details of potential actions remain under discussion.

#杨紫工作室回应AI换脸杨紫##红果回应有AI短剧使用杨紫的脸# 今日，#曝AI短剧使用杨紫的脸# 引发广泛关注。媒体就此事向红果平台核实，平台客服表示已收到相关反馈，目前涉事视频已下架处理。
随后@杨紫工作室 发布声明，明确此类AI换脸行为已严重侵犯杨紫合法权益，工作室已委托律师取证，并将通过法 ​​​

Customer-service records cited in recent coverage showed that when Yang Zi’s representatives or fans attempted to locate the disputed micro-drama, platform support staff confirmed that the title could no longer be found in the catalog. These chat transcripts indicated the complaint had been registered but that the content was already unavailable, prompting legal experts and commentators to question how broadly the AI-derived series might have been distributed prior to removal and what internal review procedures the short-video platform had applied.

The controversy surrounding the alleged misuse of actress Yang Zi’s image in AI-related video clips has been characterized by her studio as a matter of infringement of lawful rights, according to a statement released this week. The studio emphasized that any unauthorized use of her portrait, name or identity for commercial or widely disseminated content could violate personality and related rights under existing regulations. It called on producers and platforms to strengthen review mechanisms and urged users to refrain from forwarding questionable materials, while saying that relevant legal procedures may be pursued if necessary.

Media graphics and expert commentary on the Yang Zi case have depicted a streamlined industrial pipeline in which a celebrity-style portrait can be ingested by an AI face-mapping interface and then deployed across a mobile drama dashboard for serialized micro-dramas. Rights specialists noted that the combination of realistic facial cloning, automated costume generation and app-based distribution allows platforms to repurpose recognizable likenesses into low-cost, AI-driven content at scale, intensifying debates over portrait rights, consent and revenue-sharing in China’s entertainment sector.

International observers watching developments in China’s AI and entertainment regulation have cited the Yang Zi case as a prominent example of how generative technologies intersect with star-driven media economies. Comparative analyses link the controversy to global debates on deepfake performers, synthetic influencers and digital doubles, arguing that short-video platforms’ experiments with AI micro-dramas will likely shape emerging standards for consent, attribution and monetization involving the digital personas of actors and other public figures.

Commentaries in Chinese entertainment media have pointed out that the available statements from Yang Zi’s studio refer only to unauthorized use of her likeness in specific video works, rather than confirming any broader platform-produced drama franchise, according to recent reports. Analysts noted that references in the dispute focus on individual clips or short videos circulated online, and there has been no official documentation indicating that a full series or branded program involving AI-modified versions of the actress has been formally developed. Observers say further information from platforms would be needed to clarify the scope of the content involved.

Industry accounts examining the case of Yang Zi emphasized that the contested fantasy micro-drama was not merely circulating as a casual user creation but appeared within the short-video app’s vertical interface in a format indistinguishable from premium episodic releases. The series layout, episode labeling and promotional banners suggested a standardized, officially curated program featuring a lead character modeled on Yang Zi’s facial features, reinforcing concerns that the platform had integrated an unauthorized digital stand‑in into its conventional content pipeline.

Industry observers reviewing public statements related to the Yang Zi image dispute say the material released so far does not indicate that the actress herself has participated in, endorsed, licensed or negotiated any AI-driven project. According to summaries circulated by legal and entertainment commentators this week, the studio’s position centers on opposition to the unauthorized use of her likeness, without mentioning any cooperation frameworks. Analysts suggest that, in the absence of explicit contracts or endorsements, speculation about direct involvement by the performer should be treated with caution.

Technologists involved in AI development note that modern face-swapping and generative video systems can now create highly convincing replicas of public figures such as Yang Zi using relatively small training datasets. In discussions about the short-video platform case, they emphasize that user-friendly interfaces have lowered technical barriers, making it easy for producers and studios to test different celebrity likenesses in micro-dramas, which in turn raises complex questions about accountability when platform branding suggests official endorsement.

In recent reports on China’s booming short-video industry, a major mobile streaming platform was described as promoting a Chinese-style fantasy micro-drama built around a heroine closely resembling actress Yang Zi, dressed in ornate historical costume and shown within a familiar app-style interface. Observers noted that the work was marketed with a glossy, platform-branded release aesthetic typical of official serial content, raising concerns that an advanced algorithmic digital replica of Yang Zi’s likeness had been used without authorization as part of a broader commercial push for face-cloned entertainment characters.

Several Chinese entertainment news outlets and popular social media accounts reported this week that the video materials at the center of the recent dispute involving actress Yang Zi had been removed from major platforms. According to these accounts, links shared by users now display error messages or are no longer publicly accessible, suggesting that platform operators or content uploaders may have taken them down. Commentators noted that the specific timing and the party initiating the removal have not been clearly disclosed, and follow-up measures by regulators or rights holders remain to be seen.

Within the broader entertainment ecosystem, Chinese short-video providers have aggressively promoted micro-dramas and vertical series as new revenue drivers, often blending human performances with algorithmically generated backdrops and characters. Analysts observing the Yang Zi dispute suggest that the commercial incentive to create instantly familiar digital leads can encourage the replication of popular actors’ faces, and that without clear contractual frameworks, this dynamic risks normalizing unlicensed face-cloning as a routine production technique.

Consumer advocates responding to the Yang Zi incident have highlighted the perspective of ordinary viewers who encounter AI-based micro-dramas within heavily gamified short-video apps. According to recent commentary, the seamless integration of digital replicas into fantasy series, recommendation feeds and branded release pages can make it difficult for audiences to distinguish between authorized collaborations and unlicensed cloning, underscoring calls for clearer on-screen disclosures and stronger penalties for misuse of celebrity likenesses.

Legal scholars examining the controversy surrounding Yang Zi and Chinese short-video platforms have pointed to China’s evolving regulatory framework on deep synthesis technologies, including provisions that require prominent labeling of algorithmically produced content and explicit consent for the commercial use of an individual’s likeness. Commentators argue that the incident illustrates gaps between written rules and platform-level enforcement, with the rapid growth of AI micro-dramas challenging existing mechanisms for rights protection and complaint resolution.

text_analysis.alignment_reasoning: The post fabricates specific details about the drama's title and nature (calling it a 'platform-supported' series with a specific title) that are not supported by evidence, which describes only unauthorized clips and notes the lack of official documentation for a full series.
text_analysis.t1_evidence: The post claims the title was 'Reborn, I Became My Mother’s Guardian Deity' and describes it as a 'major platform’s AI series' or 'structured content rollout', whereas evidence [5] states 'there has been no official documentation indicating that a full series or branded program involving AI-modified versions of the actress has been formally developed' and evidence [1] refers only to '某作品' (a certain work) without naming a specific series title.
t1_problematic_subclaims: The title most often linked to the controversy in online discussion was 'Reborn, I Became My Mother’s Guardian Deity'
a major platform’s AI series can be assembled, packaged, distributed
text_analysis.t2_evidence: The post frames the incident as a 'failed trial run in platform-supported synthetic entertainment' and a 'commercial test balloon' by a platform, whereas evidence [5] and [6] characterize the studio's statement as referring only to 'unauthorized use of her likeness in specific video works' and note that 'speculation about direct involvement by the performer should be treated with caution', implying the post overstates the platform's intentional role.
t2_problematic_subclaims: a failed trial run in platform-supported synthetic entertainment
semi-formal AI release that a company could boost through its recommendation systems
text_analysis.global_assessment: The post significantly distorts the event by inventing a specific drama title and framing unauthorized user-generated content as a deliberate, platform-backed commercial experiment, contradicting evidence that describes the incident as isolated infringement without official series documentation.

images.scene_description: A vertical screenshot of a mobile video app interface featuring a woman in elaborate historical costume. The image has a highly polished, glossy aesthetic with stylized golden calligraphy text overlaid on the left side and standard app UI elements (like, comment, share) on the right.
images.ocr_text: 新剧推荐 | 关注 | 发现 | 推荐 | 花笑记 | 快意恩仇 · 宿命难逃 | 第1集 | 全80集 · 已更新 | @花笑记官方 | 她为家族隐忍筹谋，却在宿命中遇见了真心。#花笑记 #古装 #新剧上线 | 每日12:00更新2集 | 会员抢先看 | 热播榜TOP3 · 98.3万人在看
images.v1_evidence: The image exhibits the visual signature of a high-fidelity AI-generated promotional poster, characterized by the 'plastic' smoothness of the skin, the hyper-stylized and glowing calligraphy text, and the perfect, studio-like lighting that lacks the imperfections of a candid camera capture.
images.v2_evidence: The image is a composite of a character portrait with overlaid UI elements (app interface, text banners, and social interaction icons) added to simulate a platform release page.
images.explanation: The image is a synthetic promotional screenshot (V1) with overlaid UI elements (V2) depicting the disputed drama.

images.scene_description: A screenshot of a social media post from 'Yang Zi Studio' (杨紫工作室). It features a profile picture, a verified badge, a timestamp, and a block of text issuing a legal objection regarding unauthorized AI face-swapping.
images.ocr_text: 杨紫工作室 | 当事方 | 26-3-20 16:33 | 演员杨紫工作室官博 | 发布于 北京 | 针对某作品未经授权，利用 AI 换脸、深度合成等技术，擅自使用杨紫女士的肖像制作并传播视频内容的行为，严重侵犯杨紫女士合法权益。工作室已委托律师对侵权内容进行取证，并将通过法律途径追究到底！目前相关视频已下架。技术可以创造美好未来，但不该成为侵权的工具。网络不是法外之地，工作室坚决维护艺人权益，绝不姑息任何侵权行为！
images.explanation: Clean screenshot of the official studio statement.

images.scene_description: A vertical screenshot of a video player interface showing a woman in historical costume with a distressed expression. The image has a cinematic, high-contrast look. Text overlays indicate the episode title and a disclaimer.
images.ocr_text: 第14集 | 倍速 | 重生后 我成了娘亲的守护神 | 人生逆转 守护所爱 | EP14 逆天改命 | 18.99 登录领钱 | 3.7万 | 177 | 981 | 639 | 重生后，我成了娘亲的守护神 > | 第14集 | 逆天改命 | 热评 | 哭的时候好像杨紫 | 作者声明：内容由AI生成 | 官方独播 | 画质增强
images.v1_evidence: The image displays the visual characteristics of AI-generated video content, including the hyper-realistic yet slightly uncanny texture of the skin and makeup, and is explicitly labeled with the text 'Author statement: Content generated by AI' (作者声明：内容由AI生成).
images.v2_evidence: The image is a composite of the video frame with overlaid UI elements such as the episode title, engagement metrics, and the AI disclaimer text.
images.explanation: The image is a synthetic video frame (V1) with overlaid UI elements (V2) from the disputed drama.

images.scene_description: A screenshot of a chat interface with an online customer service agent. The chat bubbles contain text discussing a complaint about a short drama and its removal.
images.ocr_text: 在线客服 | 平台已收到你反馈的情况，为方便平台核实相关问题，请按如下投诉材料标准补正补齐投诉材料并提交至邮箱 hongguojubao@bytedance.com。平台将在收到你的完整投诉材料后及时核查处理。感谢你的关注与支持！ | 欢迎回来，请继续和在线客服进行咨询 | 现在这个短剧搜不到了，是什么情况 | 可能下架了哦 | 还有其他可以帮到你的吗~

images.scene_description: A complex infographic titled 'The Clone Economy'. It features a woman's face with a facial landmark mesh overlay, a 'Deepfake Generation' interface mockup, and a mobile app dashboard mockup. The style is highly polished, glossy, and illustrative.
images.ocr_text: COVER STORY | The Clone Economy | Inside Hollywood's rush to build—and brand—AI versions of stars. | FACIAL LANDMARK MAPPING | SIMILARITY SCORE 98.7% | MODEL CONFIDENCE HIGH | DEEPFAKE GENERATION | SOURCE FOOTAGE | AI GENERATED | STARCLONE | EXCLUSIVE SERIES | ECHOES Ep. 1 | ECHOES Ep. 2 | ECHOES Ep. 3 | BEHIND THE SCENES | FAN INTERACTIONS
images.v1_evidence: The image is a synthetic editorial illustration/infographic, evidenced by the stylized 'facial landmark mapping' mesh, the mock-up software interfaces, and the glossy, non-photorealistic rendering of the subject and devices.
images.v2_evidence: The image is a composite graphic design piece combining multiple visual elements (portrait, UI mockups, text blocks) into a single illustrative canvas.
images.explanation: The image is a synthetic infographic (V1) and composite design (V2) illustrating the concept of AI cloning.

image_analysis.global_notes: The post contains a mix of clean screenshots (images 2, 4, 6) and synthetic/composite images (images 1, 3, 5). Images 1 and 3 are AI-generated content with UI overlays, while image 5 is a synthetic infographic. All images align with the post's narrative about AI-generated drama and the subsequent legal/platform response.

judge.level3_rationale: 虽然杨紫工作室确实就AI换脸侵权事件发声并导致视频下架，但文章将此事描述为平台支持的“结构化内容发布”属于误导性扭曲，且配图包含大量AI生成的合成视觉内容，导致整体真实性受损。
judge.reasoning_trace: The post reports a real event (Yang Zi AI drama controversy) but distorts the nature of the release (claiming it was a platform-supported rollout rather than just unauthorized clips) and uses synthetic visuals (V1) to support the narrative. V1 presence forces the verdict below Mostly True. The distortion of the event's scale/nature (T2) combined with V1 leads to Mostly False.
judge.core_claim: An unauthorized AI-generated micro-drama featuring actress Yang Zi's likeness was distributed on Chinese short-video platforms, prompting a legal objection from her studio and subsequent takedown of the content.
key_subclaims: Yang Zi's studio issued a public objection on March 20 regarding unauthorized AI face-swapping of her likeness.
The studio stated that lawyers were instructed to preserve evidence and pursue liability.
The disputed videos were taken down from the platforms.
The specific title of the controversial drama was 'Reborn, I Became My Mother’s Guardian Deity'.
The incident represents a 'platform-supported' or 'structured content rollout' of synthetic entertainment.

subclaim_findings.subclaim: Yang Zi's studio issued a public objection on March 20 regarding unauthorized AI face-swapping of her likeness.
subclaim_findings.note: Image 2 and evidence [1] confirm the studio's statement and the date.

subclaim_findings.subclaim: The studio stated that lawyers were instructed to preserve evidence and pursue liability.
subclaim_findings.note: Image 2 text explicitly mentions entrusting lawyers to collect evidence and pursue legal action.

subclaim_findings.subclaim: The disputed videos were taken down from the platforms.
subclaim_findings.note: Image 4 and evidence [4] confirm the platform customer service stated the video was taken down.

subclaim_findings.subclaim: The specific title of the controversial drama was 'Reborn, I Became My Mother’s Guardian Deity'.
subclaim_findings.note: Image 3 shows the title '重生后 我成了娘亲的守护神' which translates to the claimed title.

subclaim_findings.subclaim: The incident represents a 'platform-supported' or 'structured content rollout' of synthetic entertainment.
subclaim_findings.note: Evidence [9] suggests the studio only referred to specific video works, and there is no official documentation of a full platform-produced franchise, making the 'platform-supported' framing speculative.

judge.verdict_reasoning: The core claim that Yang Zi's likeness was used in an unauthorized AI drama and subsequently removed is fully supported by evidence (studio statement, takedown confirmation). However, the post frames this as a 'platform-supported' or 'structured content rollout' (T2 Distortion), which is speculative and not confirmed by evidence that only cites specific video works. Additionally, the post includes AI-generated promotional images and infographics (V1, V2) to illustrate the story. Since the core facts are true but the narrative framing is distorted and synthetic visuals are present, the verdict is Mostly False due to the presence of V1 (which mandates at least Mostly False) and the speculative nature of the 'platform rollout' claim.

image_findings.explanation: The image is a high-fidelity AI-generated promotional poster (V1) with overlaid UI elements (V2) illustrating the 'sheen of a proper platform debut' described in the text.
Authentic screenshot of the studio's Weibo post confirming the legal objection.
The image shows the AI-generated drama content (V1) with app interface overlays (V2), matching the text's description of the disputed video.
Authentic screenshot of customer service chat confirming the takedown.
Synthetic infographic (V1) and composite design (V2) illustrating the 'Clone Economy' concept discussed in the text.
Duplicate of the customer service chat screenshot.
